meaning: n.电版,电版印刷物vt.制成电版vi.制电版
examples: A sheet of metal, plastic, rubber, paperboard, or other material prepared for use as a printing surface, such as an electrotype or a stereotype.
The Rapid Electrotype Company will be central in the definition of the new building not only from a programmatic-distributive point of view, but also physically.
Electrotype: a duplicate relief printing plate made electrolytically by depositing copper on a wax mould taken from an original plate or type and backing it with a lead alloy.
